title: Living in Boulder vlog 6 - 23 - 2018: Putting audio books on my iPhone X 256gb

enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs about setting up his new iPhone X (256GB) to serve as an all-in-one audiobook and podcast device. He explains his large collection of ~1500 audiobooks from LibriVox and Kindle, the process of transferring MP3s via iTunes playlists, and his use of the Natural Reader app to listen to text from his history website. He also mentions accidentally deleting part of his audiobook collection and relying on Carbonite for partial recovery.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Amazon's failure to allow Kindle books to play on iPhone is a huge mistake that will make them obsolete
A 256GB iPhone can finally serve as a complete portable audiobook and media player, replacing dedicated MP3 players
Has collected every history, philosophy, and religion audiobook from LibriVox and considers the collection nearly complete
Worth the $10 purchase; useful for reading his website's text content aloud at adjustable speeds

claims.claim: He lacks motivation to do any work until he figures out his current situation.

claims.claim: He has about 89 gigabytes of audiobooks but suspects his collection is incomplete.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He can organize his audiobooks by date, size, and title on his website.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He accidentally deleted some files and had to recover them from Carbonite.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He now has gigabit internet, which makes downloading large files faster.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He plans to organize his audiobooks into playlists on iTunes.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He aims to finish transferring his audiobook collection to his iPhone in the next couple of days.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He mentions being high while making the video.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He has a 3000-page book divided into 33 sections.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He can adjust the reading speed on Natural Reader to match his preference.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He lost some files due to a mishap with external hard drives.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He recalls screaming for half an hour after losing files, which alarmed his neighbor.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He is working on adding more years to his audiobook collection, starting from 1900 to 2000.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He hopes to eventually include books from ancient times up to the year 500.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He believes future computers will make managing his audiobook collection easier.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He plans to create playlists for his audiobooks and music on iTunes.
